## Facility Tier Rating: **Tier III**

Declared in the project ledger (`facility_rating_declared`: **Tier III`).

### Why it is rated Tier III

The design basis and specifications mandate the following, which are the hallmarks of a Uptime Institute **Tier III** (Concurrently Maintainable) facility:

| Requirement | Evidence |
|---|---|
| **Uptime Institute Tier III + TIA-942 compliance** | *"Electrical design shall meet TIER III Guidelines of the Uptime Institute and TIA942 standards except DG sets"* — (HPC-BYOH_Technical_Doc.pdf p.41) |
| **Redundant & concurrently maintainable capacity components** | *"Redundancy and concurrent maintainability are must for all the capacity components (TRANSFORMER, UPS, DG, HT & LT Panels, etc.)"* — (HPC-BYOH_Technical_Doc.pdf p.41) |
| **N+1 / N+2 electrical topology** | UPS system modules specified as **N+2** (spec_26_33_53.pdf p.2); redundancy takeover testing verifies **N+1** (spec_26_33_53.pdf p.7) |
| **Redundant LT panels per Tier III** | *"Redundant and concurrent maintainability shall be provided for UPS, Precision Cooling and Safety and Security systems LT panels as per Tier III"* — (HPC-BYOH_Technical_Doc.pdf p.41) |
| **Vendor experience requirement** | UPS and static equipment vendors must have *"at least 5 reference installations in Tier III or higher"* — (spec_26_33_53.pdf p.1, spec_26_05_00.pdf p.2) |
| **Standby fuel autonomy: 48 hours** | 100,000 litres per tank — (spec_26_32_13.pdf p.4) |
| **Target PUE: 1.45** | — (design_basis_report.pdf p.1) |
| **5-level commissioning (L1–L5, 40 tests)** | — (facility page, commissioning register) |

### Gaps / items not yet corroborated in the ledger

- **Cooling redundancy topology** — not declared in any parsed document.
- **Concurrent maintainability / fault-tolerance language** — not found as explicit resilience clauses.

These are marked as **open gaps** on the facility page.
